name: supervisord
explanation: Supervisord is monitored via its XML-RPC interface, reachable either through an inet HTTP server (default `http://localhost:9001/RPC2`) or through a UNIX domain socket whose path is deployment-specific. The only spec-required field is `name`, an arbitrary tag chosen by the user. Two things make this non-generic: (1) the typical production deployment uses a UNIX socket whose path cannot be guessed without parsing supervisor's own config, and (2) when authentication is enabled in `inet_http_server`, calls return 401 unless `username`/`password` are supplied. The HTTP+no-auth case (port 9001) is probable, but real deployments often need socket-path discovery or credentials.
notes: The `name` field is just a friendly tag — not a discovery input. Discovery would have to (a) probe TCP 9001 for the XML-RPC endpoint, (b) fall back to enumerating common socket paths (e.g. /var/run/supervisor.sock, /tmp/supervisor.sock), and (c) give up if auth is required. Closest to pattern E (auth-optional but practically required in some deployments) combined with socket-path discovery.
